title: pi-chart event envelope
description: One clinical claim/event envelope. Atomic structured events go in events.ndjson; narrative notes use frontmatter that conforms to note.schema.json and must have a matching communication event. Clinical types (observation/assessment/intent/action/communication/artifact_ref) require encounter_id, certainty, data, and links in addition to the base envelope. Structural types (subject/encounter/constraint_set) require only the base envelope.

id.description: Stable, unique identifier. Conventions (enforced by linting, not schema): evt_YYYYMMDDTHHMM[_suffix] for atomic events, note_YYYYMMDDTHHMM[_slug] for narrative notes. Structural entities (patient, encounter, constraint_set) use domain ids like 'patient_001', 'enc_001'.

type.description: Closed set. The first six are clinical claim types per CLAIM-TYPES.md. The last three are chart-structural types used by patient.md, constraints.md, and encounter headers.

subtype.description: Open-ended, snake_case. See CLAIM-TYPES.md for conventional values per type.

subject.description: Patient identifier. Must match chart.yaml subject.

encounter_id.description: Encounter this event belongs to. Required for clinical events; optional for subject/constraint_set.

effective_at.description: When the claim was/is true in the world.

recorded_at.description: When the claim was written to the chart.

run_id.description: For agent authors, the decision-cycle run identifier.

kind.description: Provenance channel, e.g. monitor_extension, patient_statement, agent_inference, agent_reasoning, agent_bedside_observation, agent_synthesis, agent_action, admission_intake, admission_order, lab_instrument.

ref.description: Source-specific reference, e.g. a run_id or instrument id.

certainty.description: Epistemic grade of the claim itself.

status.description: Lifecycle of the claim within the chart.

data.description: Type-specific payload. See CLAIM-TYPES.md for conventional shapes.

oneOf.description: Bare string form. Either an event/note id (e.g. 'evt_20260418T0815_01' or 'note_20260418T0845_sbar') OR a vitals interval URI of the form 'vitals://<encounter_id>?name=<metric>&from=<iso8601>&to=<iso8601>[&unit=<unit>]'.

Structured EvidenceRef. Use kind:event/note/artifact for explicit record references and kind:vitals for encounter-scoped trend windows. Kind-specific required fields (id for event/note/artifact; metric/from/to for vitals) are enforced by the validator.

supports.description: Evidence supporting this claim. Mix of bare ids / vitals:// URIs (back-compat) and structured EvidenceRef objects (canonical for vitals windows and explicit note/artifact references). For assessments, validator rules still require at least one observation, vitals window, or artifact_ref among the supports.

supersedes.description: IDs of events this claim replaces.

corrects.description: IDs of events flagged as erroneous and replaced by this one.

fulfills.description: IDs of intent events this action or outcome fulfills. Validator enforces that targets are type:'intent' within the same patient.

addresses.description: IDs of problems (or, when chained via an intent, the intent itself) this claim addresses. Validator enforces target types.
